rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MELVIN UHR et al VS. AMERICAN LAFRANCE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Litchfield Cavo LLP¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant American LaFrance, LLC is granted. Counsel demonstrated proper grounds to be allowed to withdraw. Plaintiffs¿ request that the Court order counsel to provide insurance information or order counsel to respond to discovery that has not been propounded is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
